signals.reasoning: BTC is liquid and stable, but the board still marks it no-trade and funding is positive. With macro still RED, there is not enough directional edge to justify fresh exposure.
ETH remains soft and directionless, with positive funding and a no-trade board read. It is neither a strong long nor a strong short here.
SOL is mildly constructive on price, but funding is positive and the board still says no-trade. Better to leave it alone than force a mediocre setup.

TURBO has one of the strongest squeeze profiles on the sheet: strong 24h momentum, deeply negative funding, and elite squeeze plus crowding scores. Confidence is capped by thin liquidity and macro still being RED, so this is only a reduced-size momentum buy.
ZEC has decent liquidity and mild positive price action, but the directional edge is still weak and the board is no-trade. Not compelling enough to deploy capital.
VVV has strong price momentum and acceptable liquidity, but the mechanical board is still neutral and funding is positive rather than supportive of a squeeze. Interesting, but not top-tier.

MON is the cleaner bearish name on this board: slightly negative 24h performance, positive funding, real liquidity, and a bearish mechanical lean. It is not a huge edge, but it is the better short relative to the rest of the sheet.
TON is orderly and liquid enough, but the setup is still neutral and funding remains positive. Watchlist only.
GRIFFAIN has some upside follow-through, but the board is mixed and execution quality is only moderate. Not cleaner than the stronger squeeze candidates.

TNSR remains one of the best continuation longs on the board with strong 24h expansion, negative funding, and elite squeeze characteristics. Macro still being RED keeps this as a reduced-size trade rather than a full-risk chase.

BLUR still has strong squeeze-continuation qualities with solid momentum, negative funding, and better liquidity than many other small caps. Confidence is capped by the wider spread and the still-risk-off macro backdrop.
